candidate_problem_record.subject: 物理
candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: Refer to Fig. 3.25. The switch is suddenly moved to position $B$. Just after the switching, what are currents in $\varepsilon_{2}$, $R_{1}$, $R_{2}$ and $L$ ?
candidate_problem_record.multi_solution_policy_rationale: 该数据集保留多解潜力评估，但不默认强推多解 agent。

extraction_notes: image entries are PIL object string representations rather than concrete file paths or URLs
metadata.reasoning_chain: When the switch is suddenly moved to position $B$, $I_{L}$ holds constant instantaneously, namely, $I_{L}=0.5\,\mathrm{mA}$ and flows downward. Let the currents through $R_{1}$ and $R_{2}$ be $I_{R_{1}}$ and $I_{R_{2}}$ and their directions be rightward and upward respectively. Now we have $$\left\{\begin{array}{l} I_{R_{1}}+0.5\times10^{-3}=I_{R_{2}} \\ I_{R_{1}}R_{1}+I_{R_{2}}R_{2}=(I_{R_{1}}+I_{R_{2}})\times10^{4}=\varepsilon_{2}=10 \end{array}\right.$$ Solving these equations we have $$\begin{array}{ll} I_{R_{1}}=0.25\,\mathrm{mA},\ \text{rightward} ; & I_{R_{2}}=0.75\,\mathrm{mA},\ \text{upward} \\ I_{\varepsilon_{2}}=0.25\,\mathrm{mA},\ \text{downward} ; & I_{L}=0.5\,\mathrm{mA},\ \text{downward} \end{array}$$
